arxiv.title: Bike Frames: Understanding the Implicit Portrayal of Cyclists in the News
arxiv.abstract: Increasing cycling for transportation or recreation can boost health and reduce the environmental impacts of vehicles. arxiv.title: CNN-Based Action Recognition and Pose Estimation for Classifying Animal Behavior from Videos: A Survey
arxiv.abstract: Classifying the behavior of humans or animals from videos is important in biomedical fields for understanding brain function and response to stimuli. Action recognition, classifying activities performed by one or more subjects in a trimmed video, forms the basis of many of these techniques. Deep lea

arxiv.title: Inpainting borehole images using Generative Adversarial Networks
arxiv.abstract: In this paper, we propose a GAN-based approach for gap filling in borehole images created by wireline microresistivity imaging tools. The proposed method utilizes a generator, global discriminator, and local discriminator to inpaint the missing regions of the image. The generator is based on an auto

arxiv.title: CORE: Learning Consistent Ordinal REpresentations for Image Ordinal Estimation
arxiv.abstract: The goal of image ordinal estimation is to estimate the ordinal label of a given image with a convolutional neural network. Existing methods are mainly based on ordinal regression and particularly focus on modeling the ordinal mapping from the feature representation of the input to the ordinal label

arxiv.title: Learning to Compress Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) Captured Video: Benchmark and Analysis
arxiv.abstract: During the past decade, the Unmanned-Aerial-Vehicles (UAVs) have attracted increasing attention due to their flexible, extensive, and dynamic space-sensing capabilities. arxiv.title: Analogy between Boltzmann machines and Feynman path integrals
arxiv.abstract: We provide a detailed exposition of the connections between Boltzmann machines commonly utilized in machine learning problems and the ideas already well known in quantum statistical mechanics through Feynman's description of the same. arxiv.title: CogReact: A Reinforced Framework to Model Human Cognitive Reaction Modulated by Dynamic Intervention
arxiv.abstract: Using deep neural networks as computational models to simulate cognitive process can provide key insights into human behavioral dynamics. Challenges arise when environments are highly dynamic, obscuring stimulus-behavior relationships. However, the majority of current research focuses on simulating

arxiv.title: What artificial intelligence might teach us about the origin of human language
arxiv.abstract: This study explores an interesting pattern emerging from research that combines artificial intelligence with sound symbolism. arxiv.title: Efficiently Computing Sparse Fourier Transforms of $q$-ary Functions
arxiv.abstract: Fourier transformations of pseudo-Boolean functions are popular tools for analyzing functions of binary sequences. arxiv.title: Doubly Robust Counterfactual Classification
arxiv.abstract: We study counterfactual classification as a new tool for decision-making under hypothetical (contrary to fact) scenarios.
